overall_summary: Analysed 94 clauses across 5 categories in Discord Inc. Terms of Service. Found 10 critical risks, 55 moderate concerns, and 14 positive clauses. This document has some areas of concern worth reviewing.

clauses.clause_text: We also have a Privacy Policy ... and other policies ... that apply to your use of our services and are incorporated into these terms.
clauses.summary: Discord says its Privacy Policy is part of the contract, so privacy rules outside this Terms document still bind you.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, the real details about your data handling may be in a separate Privacy Policy that also governs you.

clauses.clause_text: We may take additional steps, including the use of third-party services, to determine whether you are old enough to create a Discord account or access certain features or spaces.
clauses.summary: Discord may use outside services to verify your age.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can send information about you to third-party age-check providers or use extra verification data to assess your age.
clauses.section_reference: Age requirements and responsibility of parents and legal guardians

clauses.clause_text: When Discord is running, we may identify active games and other applications on your device to allow you to quickly call a friend while in a game, stream what you’re doing on your device, share your status (including what game you're playing or what song you’re listening to if you link a music account), and more.
clauses.summary: Discord can detect what games and other apps are active on your device and use linked-account activity like music listening status.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can monitor app activity on your device to power status and sharing features, which is broader device-level collection than just chat data.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: You can change your Settings at any time, and you’re in control of whether or not you connect other accounts to Discord.
clauses.summary: Discord says linked accounts and related sharing features are under your control.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you can choose whether to connect outside accounts and can change those settings later.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: Discord’s services may be personalized to each user based on a variety of factors, including their activity, preferences they identified in their Settings, and servers they belong to, so that you can see content and communities that may be of interest to you (such as features or activity on Discord, promotions from us, and sponsored content).
clauses.summary: Discord personalizes what you see using your activity, settings, and server memberships, including promotions and sponsored content.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can profile your behavior and interests to shape recommendations, promotions, and ads-like sponsored material.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: You can control whether and to what extent Discord personalizes your experience in your Settings.
clauses.summary: Discord says you can adjust personalization controls in settings.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may be able to reduce or limit some profiling and recommendation features yourself.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: Certain features and settings may have different defaults depending on your age or where you live, and some may not be optional at all.
clauses.summary: Discord may apply age- or location-based privacy and safety settings automatically, and some cannot be turned off.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can process age and location context to enforce mandatory settings you may not be able to change.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: You can provide a username and password, and a way of contacting you (such as an email address and/or phone number). You’ll also need to provide your birthday. In some cases, you may be required to verify your account or provide additional information.
clauses.summary: Discord collects account identifiers and contact details, plus your date of birth, and may ask for more information later.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can require personal details beyond basic signup information, including age-related data and verification information.

clauses.clause_text: If you get locked out of your account, we’ll need to contact you at the email or phone number associated with your account.
clauses.summary: Discord uses your saved contact details for account recovery.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can rely on your email or phone number to verify and restore account access.

clauses.clause_text: Learn more about how we use your content in our Privacy Policy
clauses.summary: Discord does not fully explain content-data handling here and points you to the Privacy Policy for those details.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, how your messages, uploads, and other content are processed may be governed by a separate policy not fully described in this text.

clauses.clause_text: You agree that we may disclose your content to third parties outside of the services when we believe we’re required to do so by law or regulation or that doing so is necessary to prevent harm to a person or to protect Discord’s rights and property.
clauses.summary: Discord can share your content with outside parties for legal compliance, safety, or to protect itself.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can hand over your messages or uploads to external parties when they think law, safety, or company protection requires it.

clauses.clause_text: You should review any terms and policies provided by the third parties before doing so as they govern your use of their services... Discord is not responsible for any third-party services.
clauses.summary: Third-party apps, bots, and integrations have their own privacy rules, and Discord disclaims responsibility for them.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, bots and third-party apps in servers may collect or use your data under their own policies, not Discord’s.

clauses.clause_text: Disabling your account limits the processing of your personal information as described in our Privacy Policy. Disabling your account does not terminate this agreement.
clauses.summary: Disabling an account reduces data processing, but does not fully end the relationship or necessarily delete data.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, turning your account off may not erase your data; it may only reduce some processing as described elsewhere.

clauses.clause_text: Our rights to retain and display certain data
clauses.summary: Discord says some data may be kept and still shown even after termination.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, deleting or ending your account may not remove all of your data from Discord’s systems or services.

clauses.clause_text: Where required, we’ll get your consent before sending you direct marketing, and we’ll make it easy for you to opt out.
clauses.summary: Discord promises consent-based marketing where the law requires it and says opting out will be easy.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you should have a clearer choice over promotional messages in places where consent is legally required.

categories.summary: This Terms of Service text contains several meaningful privacy-related points, but many important details are pushed into Discord’s separate Privacy Policy. The main findings here are: Discord collects core account data including email/phone and birthdate; may use third parties for age checks; can detect active games and other apps on your device; personalizes content, promotions, and sponsored content based on your activity and server memberships; and can disclose your content to outside parties for legal, safety, or self-protection reasons. There are some user-friendly controls, including settings for linked accounts and personalization, and a promise of consent/opt-out for direct marketing where required. However, account disabling is not the same as deletion, and Discord explicitly reserves rights to retain certain data after termination.
key_concerns: Discord says its Privacy Policy is part of the contract, so privacy rules outside this Terms document still bind you.
Discord may use outside services to verify your age.
Discord can detect what games and other apps are active on your device and use linked-account activity like music listening status.

clauses.clause_text: If you’re a parent or legal guardian, and you allow your child ... to use the services, then you and the minor child accept and agree to be bound by these terms and are responsible for all use of the Discord account or services, including your child’s activity on the services and purchases made by them, whether the minor’s account is now open or created later.
clauses.summary: Parents can be held financially responsible for purchases a child makes through Discord.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, Discord can treat purchases made by your child on the account as your responsibility.
clauses.section_reference: Age requirements and responsibility of parents and legal guardians

clauses.clause_text: We may assume that any communications we’ve received from your account or the associated contact information have been made by you, and that any purchases made using your account were made by you.
clauses.summary: Discord says it can treat purchases made through your account as authorized by you.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can charge purchases to your account and treat them as yours unless you can prove otherwise.

clauses.clause_text: Discord’s Paid Services Terms also apply to any purchase you make using Discord’s supported purchase flows, and you may also be asked to agree to separate terms such as our Monetization Terms before purchasing or selling new offerings through Discord.
clauses.summary: Any paid purchase is governed by separate payment terms that are not included in this text.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, important billing, refund, renewal, and fee rules may come from separate terms you need to read before buying anything.

clauses.clause_text: You are responsible for any mobile charges that you may incur for using our services. This includes data charges and charges for messaging, such as SMS, MMS, or other messaging protocols or technologies.
clauses.summary: You have to pay your own carrier charges for using Discord, including data and text-message fees.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can require you to cover any phone or mobile data costs caused by using the service.

clauses.clause_text: If Discord is the Party initiating an arbitration against you, Discord will pay all costs associated with the arbitration, including the entire filing fee.
clauses.summary: If Discord starts arbitration, Discord—not you—pays the arbitration costs.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they cannot make you cover the filing and arbitration fees when they are the one bringing the claim.

clauses.clause_text: If you initiate an arbitration against Discord, costs will be determined in accordance with the Rules for the applicable arbitration provider, to the extent allowed by law. In all arbitrations, unless otherwise required by law or the arbitration provider’s rules, you are responsible for all other additional arbitration costs incurred, including attorney’s fees and expert witness costs.
clauses.summary: If you bring a dispute, you may have to pay arbitration fees and your own extra costs like lawyers and experts.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, pursuing a claim against Discord could cost you money even before you win or lose.

clauses.clause_text: At least 14 days before the date set for an arbitration hearing, any Party may serve an offer in writing upon the other Party to allow judgment on specified terms. If the offer made by the offering Party is not accepted by the other Party, and the other Party fails to obtain a more favorable award, the other Party will not recover its post-offer costs and will pay the offering Party’s costs from the time of the offer.
clauses.summary: If you reject a settlement offer in arbitration and do worse later, you may have to pay the other side’s later costs.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, turning down an offer and then getting a worse result could leave you owing Discord’s post-offer costs.

clauses.clause_text: Any amounts owed will remain due;
clauses.summary: Ending the agreement does not erase money you still owe Discord.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can still pursue unpaid charges after your account or use of the service ends.

clauses.clause_text: If the app fails to conform to any applicable warranty, you may notify Apple and Apple will refund the app purchase price to you (if applicable) and, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Apple will have no other warranty obligation whatsoever with respect to the Discord app.
clauses.summary: For App Store purchases, Apple may refund the app purchase price if the app fails an applicable warranty.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, there is at least a stated refund path for the app purchase price through Apple where applicable.

categories.summary: This text contains only limited direct billing terms. The main financial points are that parents are responsible for minors’ purchases, Discord may treat purchases made through your account as authorized by you, mobile/data charges are your responsibility, unpaid amounts survive termination, and any actual purchases are governed by separate Paid Services Terms not included here. The dispute section also creates possible out-of-pocket costs if you bring arbitration and reject a settlement offer.
key_concerns: Parents can be held financially responsible for purchases a child makes through Discord.
Discord says it can treat purchases made through your account as authorized by you.
Any paid purchase is governed by separate payment terms that are not included in this text.

clauses.clause_text: Your content is yours, but you give us a license (which is a form of permission) to it when you use Discord.
clauses.summary: Discord says you keep ownership of what you post rather than transferring it to them.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you still legally own your messages, images, videos, and other content you upload.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: By using our services, you grant us a license to use, reproduce, distribute, create derivative works of, display, and perform your content.
clauses.summary: You give Discord broad permission to copy, share, modify, and show your content.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can use your content in many ways needed to run and change the service, including making modified versions of it.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: The rights you grant in this license are for the limited purpose of providing, developing, and improving our services as permitted by applicable laws.
clauses.summary: The license is broad, but Discord says it is limited to running, building, and improving Discord.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they cannot rely on this clause to claim unlimited unrelated commercial use of your content; the stated use is tied to the service.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: This license is worldwide, non-exclusive (which means you can still license your content to others), royalty-free (which means there are no fees for this license), sublicensable, and transferable.
clauses.summary: Discord’s license applies globally, costs them nothing, and can be passed on to others or transferred.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can let other parties use your content for Discord-related purposes and can transfer that permission as part of business arrangements.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: We reserve the right to block, remove, and/or permanently delete your content if we determine it is in breach of these terms, our Community Guidelines, our other policies, or any applicable law or regulation, or if it creates risk for Discord or negatively impacts the experience or interests of other Discord users to continue to make it available.
clauses.summary: Discord can remove or permanently delete your content based on its own judgment about rules, risk, or user experience.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can take down your posts or uploads even when the standard is broad and discretionary.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: You agree that we may disclose your content to third parties outside of the services when we believe we’re required to do so by law or regulation or that doing so is necessary to prevent harm to a person or to protect Discord’s rights and property.
clauses.summary: Discord may share your content outside the platform in limited legal or protective situations.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can hand your content to outside parties when they say law, safety, or protection of Discord requires it.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: By sending us feedback, you grant us a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, transferable license to use the feedback and ideas generated from the feedback without any restrictions, attribution, or compensation to you.
clauses.summary: If you send feedback or ideas, Discord gets a permanent, irrevocable right to use them forever without paying or crediting you.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can take your suggestions, build on them, transfer those rights, and never owe you money or attribution.
clauses.section_reference: Content in Discord’s services — Your content

clauses.clause_text: Other people’s content is theirs and doesn’t necessarily reflect Discord’s own views. You may not use this content without that person’s consent, or as allowed by law.
clauses.summary: Discord recognizes that users keep ownership of their own content and others cannot freely reuse it.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, your content is treated as belonging to you, and other users do not automatically get permission to reuse it.

clauses.clause_text: Some of our services allow you to download client software. So long as you comply with these terms, we grant you a worldwide, non-exclusive, personal, non-transferable, non-sublicensable, and non-assignable license to download, install, and run that software, solely to access our services.
clauses.summary: You only get a limited personal license to use Discord’s software; you do not own it.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you can use the app to access Discord, but you cannot treat the software itself as yours to transfer or reuse.
clauses.section_reference: Software in Discord’s services — License to our software

clauses.clause_text: You may not copy, modify, create derivative works based upon, distribute, sell, lease, or sublicense any of our software or services.
clauses.summary: Discord keeps tight control over its own software and forbids you from reusing or remixing it.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you cannot legally republish, alter, or build on Discord’s software unless law or Discord separately allows it.
clauses.section_reference: Software in Discord’s services — License to our software

clauses.clause_text: Although we are granting you this license, we retain any intellectual property rights we have in our software and services.
clauses.summary: Discord makes clear that it keeps ownership of its software and service IP.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you get permission to use Discord’s software, not ownership of it.
clauses.section_reference: Software in Discord’s services — License to our software

clauses.clause_text: Some of Discord’s services include software subject to separate open source license terms, and your use of those services are subject to your compliance with those license terms, when applicable. We encourage you to review them, as some licenses may explicitly override these terms.
clauses.summary: Some parts of Discord may be governed by separate open-source licenses that can change the default IP rules.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, some software components may come with different rights or obligations than the main Terms, so the open-source licenses matter.
clauses.section_reference: Software in Discord’s services — Open source

clauses.clause_text: You agree not to license, sell, lend, or transfer your account, Discord username, vanity URL, or other unique identifier without our prior written approval. We also reserve the right to delete, change, or reclaim your username, URL, or other identifier.
clauses.summary: You do not fully control transferable rights in your account name or identifiers, and Discord can take them back.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you cannot treat your username or vanity URL like property you can freely sell or transfer, and Discord can reclaim it.

clauses.clause_text: You may not assign these terms to anyone else without our written consent. We may assign our rights to any of our affiliates or subsidiaries, or to any successor in interest of any business associated with our services.
clauses.summary: You cannot transfer your rights under the agreement, but Discord can transfer theirs to related companies or a buyer.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, your position under the contract is locked to you, while Discord can move its rights to another company.
clauses.section_reference: More important stuff — Waiver, severability, and assignment

clauses.clause_text: Any part of these terms that by their nature should survive after termination of these terms will survive. As permitted under applicable law, this includes but may not be limited to the following: Our rights to retain and display certain data
clauses.summary: Some rights over your content or data continue even after you end the agreement.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, deleting your account does not necessarily end all of Discord’s rights to keep or show some content or data.

categories.summary: This text says users keep ownership of their content, which is a meaningful protection. But Discord still gets a broad worldwide, royalty-free, sublicensable, transferable license to use and modify that content for running, developing, and improving the service, and it can remove content at its discretion. The strongest IP risk here is the feedback clause: if you send ideas or suggestions, Discord gets a perpetual, irrevocable right to use them without attribution or payment. The text also hints that some retention/display rights survive termination, but it does not clearly explain deletion timing, export rights, or any AI training use in the material provided.
key_concerns: If you send feedback or ideas, Discord gets a permanent, irrevocable right to use them forever without paying or crediting you.
You give Discord broad permission to copy, share, modify, and show your content.
Discord’s license applies globally, costs them nothing, and can be passed on to others or transferred.

clauses.clause_text: As part of these efforts, we may add or remove features, start offering new services, or stop offering some services entirely (or just in some places or for some users) if they no longer make sense from a business perspective or create risk for Discord, our users, or other third parties.
clauses.summary: Discord says it can change features or shut down parts of the service, including for only some users or regions, when it decides that makes business or risk sense.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can reduce or discontinue features you rely on without promising that the service will stay the same.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: While we try to avoid disruptions, we cannot guarantee that there will not be an outage or change to the services, and your content may not be retrievable due to such outages or changes.
clauses.summary: Discord does not promise service continuity and warns that outages or service changes could make your content inaccessible.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may lose access to content or account-related materials if the service changes or goes down.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: If your account is compromised or you no longer have access to your email account or phone number, we may not be able to restore your access to your account or the servers you’ve created.
clauses.summary: If you lose access to your login contact methods, Discord may be unable to give you your account back.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, losing your email or phone could mean permanently losing your account and any servers you created.

clauses.clause_text: You agree not to license, sell, lend, or transfer your account, Discord username, vanity URL, or other unique identifier without our prior written approval.
clauses.summary: Your account and identifiers are personal to you and cannot be transferred without Discord's permission.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can treat account sharing or transfers as a violation and act against the account.

clauses.clause_text: We also reserve the right to delete, change, or reclaim your username, URL, or other identifier.
clauses.summary: Discord can take back or change your username or other account identifiers.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can alter account identity details you rely on, even if you want to keep them.

clauses.clause_text: Your right to terminate. You’re free to stop using Discord’s services at any time and for any reason. To terminate this agreement, you may delete your Discord account through the Settings page in the Discord app (the gear icon next to your username) and discontinue use of the services.
clauses.summary: Users get a clear self-service way to end the agreement by deleting their account in app whenever they want.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you can cancel on your own without having to contact support or give a reason.

clauses.clause_text: Certain provisions of these terms will survive termination as outlined below in the “Survival” section.
clauses.summary: Ending your account does not end every part of the contract because some terms continue afterward.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, some obligations and rules will still apply even after you leave the service.

clauses.clause_text: Disabling your account limits the processing of your personal information as described in our Privacy Policy. Disabling your account does not terminate this agreement.
clauses.summary: Simply disabling your account is not the same as actually ending the contract with Discord.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, pausing or disabling your account will not fully close it or end the terms governing your use.

clauses.clause_text: Our right to terminate. Subject to applicable law, we reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account and/or your access to some or all of our services with or without notice, at our discretion for any reason
clauses.summary: Discord claims broad power to suspend or terminate your account or access, with or without notice, for any reason it chooses.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can cut off your account or parts of the service at their discretion, potentially without warning.

clauses.clause_text: or for the following reasons: You breach, or you encourage or help others to breach, these terms (including, our Community Guidelines, our other policies, or additional terms that apply to specific services).
clauses.summary: Discord can suspend or terminate accounts for violating its rules or helping others violate them.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, breaking the platform rules can lead to suspension or termination.

clauses.clause_text: We’re required to do so to comply with a legal requirement or court order.
clauses.summary: Discord can terminate or suspend accounts when the law or a court requires it.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, legal orders can force account restrictions or closure.

clauses.clause_text: We reasonably believe termination is necessary to prevent harm to you, us, other users, or third parties.
clauses.summary: Discord can act against your account based on its own reasonable belief that termination is needed to prevent harm.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can remove your access based on a broad safety judgment before any formal finding of wrongdoing.

clauses.clause_text: Your account has been inactive for more than two years.
clauses.summary: Discord can terminate accounts that have been inactive for over two years.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, leaving your account unused for two years could result in losing it.

clauses.clause_text: Continuing to allow your account to be active, giving you access to some or all services, or hosting your content creates risk for Discord, other users, or third parties.
clauses.summary: Discord can suspend or terminate access if it thinks your account, access, or hosted content creates risk.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can restrict your account based on a broad and flexible risk standard.

clauses.clause_text: However, we will give you advance notice if reasonable to do so or required by applicable law.
clauses.summary: Discord only promises advance notice when it thinks notice is reasonable or the law requires it.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may get warning before account action, but Discord does not guarantee notice in most cases.

clauses.clause_text: You can appeal any enforcement action we take under these terms or other policies, including terminations, suspensions, or content removals through this form or available in-app options.
clauses.summary: Discord offers an appeal process for account enforcement actions, including suspensions and terminations.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you have a stated path to challenge a suspension or termination instead of being stuck with the first decision.

clauses.clause_text: If you reside in the European Union, your appeal must be submitted within six months of the day the relevant decision has been notified to you.
clauses.summary: EU users get a defined six-month deadline to file an appeal.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you need to act within that window or you may lose the chance to challenge the decision.

clauses.clause_text: Any part of these terms that by their nature should survive after termination of these terms will survive. As permitted under applicable law, this includes but may not be limited to the following: Our rights to retain and display certain data
clauses.summary: Even after termination, Discord says some terms continue, including rights to keep and display certain data.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, closing your account does not necessarily mean all account-related data disappears right away.

clauses.clause_text: Updates to these terms. We may decide to update these terms: (1) to reflect changes to our services or our business, (2) for legal or regulatory reasons, (3) to prevent abuse on or of our services, or (4) to better protect or serve users of our services. If these changes materially affect your Discord use or your legal rights, we’ll give you reasonable advance notice (unless the updates are urgent). If you continue to use our services after the changes have taken effect, it means that you agree to the changes. If you don’t agree, you must stop using our services.
clauses.summary: Discord can change the terms, usually with advance notice for material changes, and your only stated option if you disagree is to stop using the service.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, the rules governing your account can change later, and continuing to use Discord counts as acceptance.

categories.summary: This text gives users a clear self-service cancellation method and an appeal process for suspensions and terminations, which are meaningful user-friendly protections. But Discord also gives itself very broad power to suspend or terminate accounts or access with or without notice, for any reason, plus for inactivity after two years and for vague 'risk' concerns. The service also reserves broad rights to change or discontinue features, does not promise continuity, and does not provide a clear post-termination data deletion or export timeline in this text.
key_concerns: Discord claims broad power to suspend or terminate your account or access, with or without notice, for any reason it chooses.
Discord says it can change features or shut down parts of the service, including for only some users or regions, when it decides that makes business or risk sense.
Discord does not promise service continuity and warns that outages or service changes could make your content inaccessible.

clauses.clause_text: We’re actively developing new features and products to improve Discord... we cannot guarantee that there will not be an outage or change to the services, and your content may not be retrievable due to such outages or changes. We are not liable for any such outages or service changes.
clauses.summary: Discord says service outages, feature changes, or lost access to content may happen, and it will not be responsible for the harm caused by those disruptions.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can deny responsibility when outages or service changes make your content unavailable or disrupt your use of Discord.
clauses.section_reference: What you can expect from us

clauses.clause_text: We take no responsibility for any of your content, and we are not responsible for others’ use of your content.
clauses.summary: Discord disclaims responsibility for problems caused by your own content or by other people reusing it.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can argue that harms tied to your posted content or others’ reuse of it are your problem, not theirs.

clauses.clause_text: You agree we will not be liable for any harm caused by that content.
clauses.summary: Discord says it is not responsible if other users’ content harms you.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can avoid liability for damage caused by offensive, false, or harmful user content you encounter on the service.

clauses.clause_text: Discord is not responsible for any third-party services... Discord does not warrant or endorse, and will not have any liability for, any third-party services.
clauses.summary: Discord disclaims warranties and liability for bots, apps, and other third-party services used through Discord.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can deny responsibility if a third-party bot, app, or integration causes loss, damage, or malfunction.

clauses.clause_text: You will indemnify and hold Discord and its officers, directors, employees and agents harmless from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, and costs (including reasonable legal and accounting fees) related to (a) your access to or use of our services or third-party services, (b) your content, (c) your violation of these terms, or (d) your negligence or willful misconduct.
clauses.summary: This is a one-sided indemnity clause that can make you pay Discord’s losses and legal costs for claims tied broadly to your use of the service, your content, or alleged rule-breaking.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can try to make you cover their legal bills and other costs if someone sues them over your use of Discord, your content, or your alleged misconduct.

clauses.clause_text: TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, DISCORD, ITS AFFILIATES, AND THEIR RESPECTIVE SUPPLIERS MAKE NO WARRANTIES, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, ABOUT THE SERVICES. THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED “AS IS.”
clauses.summary: Discord offers the service without promises that it will work properly, reliably, or meet your expectations.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can argue you had no contractual guarantee that the service would be available, functional, or suitable for your needs.

clauses.clause_text: WE ALSO DISCLAIM ANY IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, QUIET ENJOYMENT, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT, AND ANY WARRANTIES ARISING OUT OF COURSE OF DEALING OR USAGE OF TRADE.
clauses.summary: Discord specifically disclaims the standard legal warranties users often rely on when a product or service fails.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can argue you cannot rely on implied legal promises that the service is fit for ordinary use, fit for your purpose, or non-infringing.

clauses.clause_text: THE LAWS OF CERTAIN JURISDICTIONS OR STATES DO NOT ALLOW LIMITATIONS ON IMPLIED WARRANTIES. TO THE EXTENT SUCH WARRANTIES CANNOT BE DISCLAIMED UNDER THE LAWS OF YOUR JURISDICTION, WE LIMIT THE DURATION AND REMEDIES OF SUCH WARRANTIES TO THE FULL EXTENT PERMISSIBLE UNDER THOSE LAWS.
clauses.summary: Where local law does not allow Discord to fully disclaim warranties, it still tries to narrow them as much as legally possible.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can still try to cut down any warranty rights you keep under local law to the shortest and weakest version allowed.

clauses.clause_text: WE DON’T EXCLUDE OR LIMIT OUR LIABILITY TO YOU WHERE IT WOULD BE ILLEGAL TO DO SO... THIS PARAGRAPH DOESN’T AFFECT CONSUMER RIGHTS THAT CAN’T BE WAIVED OR LIMITED BY ANY CONTRACT OR AGREEMENT.
clauses.summary: Discord acknowledges that some legal rights and liability protections cannot be waived, and it preserves those where the law requires.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they still cannot take away non-waivable consumer rights or liability protections that your local law preserves.

clauses.clause_text: IN JURISDICTIONS WHERE EXCLUSIONS OR LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY ARE ALLOWED, NEITHER DISCORD, ITS AFFILIATES, NOR OUR SUPPLIERS... WILL BE LIABLE... FOR ANY INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, OR DAMAGES FOR LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUES, LOST SAVINGS, LOST BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY, LOSS OF DATA OR GOODWILL, SERVICE INTERRUPTION, COMPUTER DAMAGE OR SYSTEM FAILURE, OR THE COST OF SUBSTITUTE SERVICES OF ANY KIND... WHETHER BASED ON WARRANTY, CONTRACT, STATUTE, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), PRODUCT LIABILITY, OR ANY OTHER LEGAL THEORY...
clauses.summary: Discord broadly excludes liability for many major categories of harm, including lost data, service interruption, system damage, and even negligence-based claims where allowed.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can try to avoid paying for many of the most serious real-world losses you might suffer from using the service.

clauses.clause_text: BESIDES THE TYPES OF LIABILITY WE CANNOT LIMIT BY LAW... DISCORD LIMITS OUR LIABILITY TO YOU TO THE GREATER OF (A) THE AMOUNTS YOU HAVE PAID US IN THE THREE MONTHS BEFORE YOU FIRST ASSERT A CLAIM OR (B) $100 USD (OR THE EQUIVALENT IN YOUR LOCAL CURRENCY).
clauses.summary: Even if Discord is liable, it caps what you can recover at either what you paid in the last three months or $100, whichever is higher.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, your compensation may be capped at a very low amount even if your actual losses are much larger.

clauses.clause_text: DISCORD ISN’T LIABLE FOR THE CONDUCT OR CONTENT, WHETHER ONLINE OR OFFLINE, OF ANY USER OF OUR SERVICES.
clauses.summary: Discord says it is not responsible for harm caused by other users, whether that happens on the platform or spills into the real world.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can deny responsibility for damage caused by harassment, fraud, abuse, or other misconduct by users.

clauses.clause_text: PLEASE READ THIS SECTION CAREFULLY. IT AFFECTS YOUR LEGAL RIGHTS, INCLUDING YOUR RIGHT TO FILE OR PARTICIPATE IN A LAWSUIT IN COURT AND LIMITING YOUR RIGHTS TO RESOLVE YOUR DISPUTE AS PART OF A CLASS OR REPRESENTATIVE ACTION.
clauses.summary: Discord warns up front that its dispute terms take away important court and group-action rights.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may lose the ability to sue in court normally or join with other users in a class case.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Settling disputes between you and Discord

clauses.clause_text: This section applies to any and all disputes between you and Discord.
clauses.summary: The dispute rules are written to cover essentially every disagreement you could have with Discord.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can try to force almost any claim you have into their chosen dispute process.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Settling disputes between you and Discord

clauses.clause_text: you agree that you will reach out to us and use good faith efforts to settle the Dispute informally through negotiation before initiating a lawsuit or arbitration... This Informal Dispute Resolution process... is a prerequisite and condition precedent to commencing any formal dispute resolution proceeding.
clauses.summary: Before you can sue or arbitrate, you must go through Discord’s required pre-dispute notice and negotiation process.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can try to block or delay your case if you do not follow their exact pre-filing steps first.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Settling disputes between you and Discord - Informal Dispute Resolution

clauses.clause_text: you, along with your counsel if you have one, must be available personally for a telephone or videoconference to resolve your claims, unless Discord states in writing that you are not personally required to attend.
clauses.summary: Discord requires your personal participation in a pre-dispute meeting before formal proceedings can start.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can insist that you personally attend a meet-and-confer process before your claim can move forward.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Settling disputes between you and Discord - Informal Dispute Resolution

clauses.clause_text: Choice of Law. These terms and all Disputes between you and Discord... shall be governed by the law of the United States and the laws of California, without regard to conflicts of law provisions...
clauses.summary: Discord chooses U.S. and California law to govern disputes for most users covered by this clause.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, your dispute may be judged under California and U.S. law instead of the law where you live, unless local mandatory protections override it.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Governing Law, Jurisdiction, Class Action Waiver and Time Limitation

clauses.clause_text: Venue and Jurisdiction... the Dispute must be resolved exclusively by a court of competent jurisdiction, federal or state (including small claims court), located in the County of New Castle, Delaware, and no other court.
clauses.summary: If a dispute goes to court instead of arbitration, Discord requires it to be filed in New Castle County, Delaware.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can force court disputes into Delaware, which may be inconvenient and expensive if you live elsewhere.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Governing Law, Jurisdiction, Class Action Waiver and Time Limitation

clauses.clause_text: If you are a consumer residing in the European Union, this clause and these terms in general do not affect any mandatory consumer rights you may have under your local law... and/or these terms shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the court of Amsterdam, the Netherlands or, if you are a consumer, to a court in your domicile if in an EU Member State.
clauses.summary: EU consumers keep mandatory local consumer rights and may sue in their home EU court.
clauses.implication: If you accept this and live in the EU as a consumer, they cannot use this clause to strip away mandatory local protections or force you only into a foreign court.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Governing Law, Jurisdiction, Class Action Waiver and Time Limitation

clauses.clause_text: Class Action Waiver. You agree that any Dispute between you and Discord... may only be pursued by you on an individual basis, and you may not bring a claim as a plaintiff or a class member in a class, collective, or representative action.
clauses.summary: Discord bans you from bringing or joining class, collective, or representative actions for covered disputes.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may have to pursue claims alone even when many users were harmed in the same way.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Governing Law, Jurisdiction, Class Action Waiver and Time Limitation

clauses.clause_text: Time for Initiating a Claim. No action, regardless of form, arising out of or relating to a Dispute may be brought by either Party more than one (1) year after the cause of action has accrued.
clauses.summary: Discord shortens the time limit for bringing claims to one year.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may lose your claim if you do not act within one year, even if the normal legal deadline would be longer.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Governing Law, Jurisdiction, Class Action Waiver and Time Limitation

clauses.clause_text: Only after engaging in the Informal Dispute Resolution process... either Party may initiate binding arbitration as the sole means to resolve Disputes...
clauses.summary: For U.S. and Canadian users who do not opt out, arbitration becomes the mandatory main path for resolving disputes.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can force your dispute out of court and into private arbitration unless an exception applies or you opt out in time.

clauses.clause_text: This Agreement to Arbitrate is intended to be interpreted broadly, and it applies to all Disputes between the Parties, including: (1) claims that arose, were asserted, or involve facts occurring before the existence of this Agreement...; (2) claims that may arise after the termination...
clauses.summary: The arbitration clause is written very broadly and even reaches past and future claims.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can try to force a wide range of old, current, and future disputes into arbitration.

clauses.clause_text: the arbitrator, and not any federal, state, or local court or agency, shall have exclusive authority to resolve: (1) all Disputes; (2) whether a claim is subject to arbitration; and (3) any dispute regarding the payment of administrative or arbitrator fees...
clauses.summary: An arbitrator, not a court, usually gets to decide whether your claim must be arbitrated and related procedural issues.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may have less chance to ask a court to decide whether Discord can force your dispute into arbitration.

clauses.clause_text: ARBITRATION MEANS THAT YOU WAIVE YOUR RIGHT TO A JURY TRIAL... YOU HEREBY ACKNOWLEDGE AND AGREE THAT YOU AND DISCORD ARE EACH WAIVING THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW.
clauses.summary: By agreeing to arbitration, you give up the right to have your case decided by a jury.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can prevent you from taking covered claims to a jury trial.

clauses.clause_text: In some instances, the costs of arbitration could exceed the costs of litigation and the right to discovery may be more limited in arbitration than in court.
clauses.summary: Discord expressly warns that arbitration can be more expensive and offer less evidence-gathering than court.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, your dispute process may be costlier and give you fewer tools to obtain evidence than a lawsuit would.

clauses.clause_text: If you initiate an arbitration against Discord, costs will be determined in accordance with the Rules for the applicable arbitration provider, to the extent allowed by law. In all arbitrations, unless otherwise required by law or the arbitration provider’s rules, you are responsible for all other additional arbitration costs incurred, including attorney’s fees and expert witness costs.
clauses.summary: If you start arbitration, you may have to pay your own arbitration-related costs, including attorney and expert fees.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, bringing a claim may be expensive enough to discourage you from pursuing smaller disputes.

clauses.clause_text: Offer of Judgment... If the offer made by the offering Party is not accepted by the other Party, and the other Party fails to obtain a more favorable award, the other Party will not recover its post-offer costs and will pay the offering Party’s costs from the time of the offer.
clauses.summary: If you reject a settlement offer and do worse in arbitration, you may have to pay the other side’s later costs.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can use cost-shifting pressure to push you toward settling rather than fully pursuing your claim.

clauses.clause_text: If you are a resident of the United States and initiate arbitration, such arbitration will take place in the County where you reside... If Discord initiates arbitration, and for residents in Canada, arbitration shall be initiated in the County of New Castle, Delaware...
clauses.summary: U.S. users who file arbitration usually get a local venue, but Canadian users and users arbitrated by Discord may be pushed toward Delaware unless changed.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, the location of arbitration may still be inconvenient, especially if you are in Canada or Discord starts the case.

clauses.clause_text: the Parties hereby waive any and all jurisdictional and venue defenses otherwise available.
clauses.summary: You give up the right to object that Delaware courts are the wrong place for arbitration-related court proceedings.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can rely on your waiver to keep related court fights in Delaware even if that forum is inconvenient for you.

clauses.clause_text: You can decline this Agreement to Arbitrate... by emailing an opt-out notice to arbitration-opt-out@discord.com within 30 days of September 29, 2025 or when you first register your Discord account, whichever is later...
clauses.summary: Discord gives users a clear 30-day opt-out from the arbitration agreement.
clauses.implication: If you accept this term set, you still have a limited window to avoid mandatory arbitration by sending the required opt-out email on time.

clauses.clause_text: You or Discord may still pursue claims, if they qualify, exclusively in a small claims court in the United States on an individual basis...
clauses.summary: Small claims court remains available for qualifying disputes despite the arbitration clause.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you may still use U.S. small claims court for eligible individual disputes instead of arbitration.

clauses.clause_text: IF YOU’RE A U.S. RESIDENT... YOU AND DISCORD AGREE THAT EACH MAY BRING CLAIMS AGAINST THE OTHER ONLY IN OUR INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY... AND THE ARBITRATOR MAY AWARD RELIEF ONLY IN FAVOR OF THE INDIVIDUAL PARTY SEEKING RELIEF...
clauses.summary: The arbitration clause also blocks class or representative arbitration and limits relief to the individual claimant.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can stop you from seeking broader group-based remedies in arbitration.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Class Arbitration and Collective Relief Waiver

clauses.clause_text: Discord will provide 30 days’ notice of the date of any material changes to this Agreement to Arbitrate... If you continue to use the site after the 30th day, you agree that any unfiled claims... are subject to the revised clause.
clauses.summary: Discord can change the arbitration rules with 30 days’ notice, and continued use counts as acceptance for unfiled claims.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can revise the dispute process later and bind you to the new version if you keep using Discord and do not opt out in time.
clauses.section_reference: 16. Changes to this Agreement to Arbitrate

clauses.clause_text: Updates to these terms... If these changes materially affect your Discord use or your legal rights, we’ll give you reasonable advance notice... If you continue to use our services after the changes have taken effect, it means that you agree to the changes. If you don’t agree, you must stop using our services.
clauses.summary: Discord can change the terms, including legal-rights terms, with notice, and your continued use counts as acceptance.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, they can update the legal rules later and treat your continued use as agreement, leaving stopping use as your main way to reject them.
clauses.section_reference: More important stuff - Updates to these terms

clauses.clause_text: Apple has no obligation to furnish any maintenance and support services with respect to the Discord app. If the app fails to conform to any applicable warranty, you may notify Apple and Apple will refund the app purchase price to you (if applicable) and, to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Apple will have no other warranty obligation whatsoever with respect to the Discord app. Apple is not responsible for addressing any claims by you or any third party relating to the Discord app...
clauses.summary: For iOS users, Apple disclaims support and most warranty or claims responsibility for the Discord app.
clauses.implication: If you accept this, you generally cannot look to Apple for support or broader legal responsibility over problems with the app beyond limited refund rights where applicable.
clauses.section_reference: More important stuff - Apple App Store

categories.summary: This text contains extensive consumer-rights limitations. The biggest issues are a broad one-sided indemnity clause, sweeping warranty disclaimers, a liability cap of the greater of $100 or three months of fees, and broad exclusions for consequential losses, lost data, outages, third-party services, and other users’ conduct. For U.S. and Canadian users, the dispute section is especially important: it imposes mandatory arbitration unless you opt out within 30 days, waives jury trial rights, blocks class and representative actions, requires a pre-dispute notice and meet-and-confer process, shortens claim deadlines to one year, and often routes court-related proceedings to Delaware. There are some user-protective carve-outs, including preservation of non-waivable consumer rights, an arbitration opt-out, small-claims access, and stronger protections for EU consumers.
key_concerns: This is a one-sided indemnity clause that can make you pay Discord’s losses and legal costs for claims tied broadly to your use of the service, your content, or alleged rule-breaking.
Discord broadly excludes liability for many major categories of harm, including lost data, service interruption, system damage, and even negligence-based claims where allowed.
Discord warns up front that its dispute terms take away important court and group-action rights.

disclaimer: This analysis is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Always consult a qualified legal professional for specific legal questions or concerns.
